mammal_scientific_name: Dasypus mexicanus / Dasypus novemcinctus legacy complex
occurrence_scope: Strong Central Texas occurrence; exact Hays filter optional
soil_or_microhabitat_target: sandy/light soils vs heavy clay soils
plant_ecosystem_relevance: Soil texture changes where armadillos dig, den, and forage; affects local disturbance frequency.
locality_scope: Central Texas / Edwards Plateau / Blackland Prairies
public_note: Use as a native soil-disturbance and shelter-row; do not join to plant taxa.
source_summary: Schmidly & Bradley 2016, The Mammals of Texas, 7th ed.

mammal_scientific_name: Dasypus mexicanus / Dasypus novemcinctus legacy complex
occurrence_scope: Strong Central Texas occurrence; exact Hays filter optional
soil_or_microhabitat_target: creek banks; sandy soils; limestone cracks/crevices
plant_ecosystem_relevance: Burrows and shallow excavations can create small bare-soil patches and shelter spaces.
public_note: Most useful armadillo soil row for Hays/Edwards Plateau dashboard.
source_summary: Schmidly & Bradley 2016, The Mammals of Texas, 7th ed.

mammal_scientific_name: Dasypus mexicanus / Dasypus novemcinctus legacy complex
occurrence_scope: Strong Central Texas occurrence; exact Hays filter optional
plant_ecosystem_relevance: Foraging pits expose soil and disturb microhabitats while accessing insects and other invertebrates.
locality_scope: Texas general with Central Texas context
public_note: Connects soil moisture/hardness to invertebrate-foraging activity.
source_summary: Schmidly & Bradley 2016, The Mammals of Texas, 7th ed.

occurrence_scope: Central Texas review; strong only where GBIF or local records support occurrence
plant_ecosystem_relevance: Creates underground tunnels and raises soil; can affect roots and surface hydrology at small scale.
locality_scope: Texas general / eastern Texas; local occurrence review needed
public_note: Include only if local occurrence is accepted for Hays/Central Texas.
source_summary: Schmidly & Bradley 2016, The Mammals of Texas, 7th ed.

occurrence_scope: Central Texas review; strong only where GBIF or local records support occurrence

locality_scope: Texas general / eastern Texas; local occurrence review needed
public_note: Useful as an ecosystem-function row, but occurrence in Hays should be checked.
source_summary: Schmidly & Bradley 2016, The Mammals of Texas, 7th ed.

occurrence_scope: Low-count Central Texas occurrence; review before public map
soil_or_microhabitat_target: burrows of ground squirrels, pocket gophers, and other fossorial prey
plant_ecosystem_relevance: Creates larger soil excavations while hunting; can open bare soil and unused burrow structures.
locality_scope: Texas general; Central Texas occurrence review
public_note: Do not overstate Hays occurrence; treat as review/occasional regional mammal.
source_summary: Schmidly & Bradley 2016, The Mammals of Texas, 7th ed.

occurrence_scope: Strong Central Texas occurrence; management layer
soil_or_microhabitat_target: mud near ponds, marshes, ditches, standing water
plant_ecosystem_relevance: Wallowing creates muddy depressions and localized vegetation loss; not a native-benefit row.
locality_scope: Texas general / Central Texas management layer
public_note: Keep visible as management impact, not native relationship.
source_summary: Schmidly & Bradley 2016, The Mammals of Texas, 7th ed.

occurrence_scope: Strong Central Texas occurrence; management layer
plant_ecosystem_relevance: Rooting can uproot vegetation and disturb seedbeds; can reduce native plant establishment.
locality_scope: Texas general / Central Texas management layer
public_note: High-priority management layer; keep separate from native mammal functions.
source_summary: Schmidly & Bradley 2016, The Mammals of Texas, 7th ed.

occurrence_scope: Strong Central Texas occurrence; management layer

plant_ecosystem_relevance: Rooting can reduce number of plant species at a site; relevant to restoration monitoring.
locality_scope: Texas general / Central Texas management layer
public_note: Use as a negative edge or warning layer, not as plant-food interaction.

occurrence_scope: Strong Central Texas occurrence; management layer

occurrence_scope: Central/South Texas review; use if occurrence accepted

public_note: Good contrast row: native peccary disturbance is not equivalent to hog rooting.
source_summary: Schmidly & Bradley 2016, The Mammals of Texas, 7th ed.
